title: Garlicky Crunchy Brazilian Tadka Hash
description: A flavorful, crunchy, and spicy Brazilian-inspired dish featuring a garlicky tadka and sweet vanilla notes, perfect for brunch or breakfast. This recipe is egg-free-friendly and perfect for adventurous eaters.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a pan over medium heat and toad the rice flour, stirring constantly for 2 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds, 1/2 teaspoon of coriander seeds, 1/2 teaspoon of grains of paradise, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the onions, red bell pepper, and zucchini to the pan and cook for 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Grate the ginger over the vegetables and stir to combine.
In a small bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of cornstarch with 2 tablespoons of water until smooth.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the pan and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture has thickened.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer the mixture to a serving platter and sprinkle with black pepper to taste.
Just before serving, stir in 1/2 teaspoon of vanilla extract.
